channel.title: Orthodox Christian Sermons: Third Sunday of Great Lent
channel.description: Audio Orthodox Christian Sermons: Third Sunday of Great Lent

item.title: 3rd Sunday Of Great Lent. Veneration of the Precious Cross., What shall a man give in exchange for his soul? A question every Christian MUST answer. The Greatest Heresy of our Time., 2002
item.description: :3rd Sunday Of Great Lent. Veneration of the Precious Cross.. What shall a man give in exchange for his soul? A question every Christian MUST answer. The Greatest Heresy of our Time., 2002

item.title: 3rd Sunday of Great Lent, Veneration Of The Holy Cross, We cannot see the Kingdom of God come with power unless we follow the way of the cross., 2010-03-07
item.description: :3rd Sunday of Great Lent. Veneration Of The Holy Cross, We cannot see the Kingdom of God come with power unless we follow the way of the cross., 2010-03-07

item.title: 3rd Sunday of Great Lent, Holy Cross, Before Thy Cross We Bow Down And Worship, 2011-03-26
item.description: :3rd Sunday of Great Lent. Why do we prostrate before the Cross, even on Sunday? An explanation of the hymn before the cross: "Before Thy cross, we bow down in worship, O Master, and Thy holy resurrection, we glorify"
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :3rd Sunday of Great Lent. Why do we prostrate before the Cross, even on Sunday? An explanation of the hymn before the cross: "Before Thy cross, we bow down in worship, O Master, and Thy holy resurrection, we glorify"

item.title: 3rd Sunday of Great Lent, Holy Cross, What Shall A Man Give In Exchange For His Soul, 2011-03-27
item.description: :3rd Sunday of Great Lent. The Gospel for the Cross asks two great questions, which are *NOT* rhetorical. We must answer every momene the question "What shall a man give in exchange for his soul?" Everything in life - whatever we desire, do, think or say - is an exchange.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :3rd Sunday of Great Lent. The Gospel for the Cross asks two great questions, which are *NOT* rhetorical. We must answer every momene the question "What shall a man give in exchange for his soul?" Everything in life - whatever we desire, do, think or say - is an exchange.

item.title: 3rd Sunday of Great Lent, Epistle for Exaltation of the Holy Cross, 2012-03-17
item.description: :3rd Sunday of Great Lent. The Epistle for the Exaltation of the Cross never mentions the cross. There are many epistles that mention the cross; why was this one chosen?
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :3rd Sunday of Great Lent. The Epistle for the Exaltation of the Cross never mentions the cross. There are many epistles that mention the cross; why was this one chosen?

item.title: 3rd Sunday of Great Lent, Exaltation of the Holy Cross, 2012-03-18
item.description: :3rd Sunday of Great Lent. The entire Gospel is important, but some passages are absolutely critical to understand and follow. This is one of them. It explains what the way of the cross is, and teaches a principle that we must use literally every moment of our lives - to learn to always make a good "exchange". We explain what the Lord means when He commands us to "deny" ourselves, and what the answers to His questions: "What shall it profit a man..." and "What shall a man give in exchange for his soul?" must be. These are not rhetorical questions - you must answer them.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :3rd Sunday of Great Lent. The entire Gospel is important, but some passages are absolutely critical to understand and follow. This is one of them. It explains what the way of the cross is, and teaches a principle that we must use literally every moment of our lives - to learn to always make a good "exchange". We explain what the Lord means when He commands us to "deny" ourselves, and what the answers to His questions: "What shall it profit a man..." and "What shall a man give in exchange for his soul?" must be. These are not rhetorical questions - you must answer them.

item.title: 3rd Sunday of Great Lent, Exaltation Of The Holy Cross, 2013-04-07
item.description: :3rd Sunday of Great Lent. Immediately after solemnly commanding us in 4 different ways to take up our cross, Jesus gave what might appear to be an out of context prophesy: "There be some of them that stand here, which shall not taste of death, till they have seen the kingdom of God come with power." This prophesy was fulfilled in part by the Transfiguration, but it is much more than that; it is the consolation that we need to take up our cross. When things are difficult, we must be viscerally aware of our the divinity and humanity of our Lord. The Gospel tells us of His divinity, and the epistle of His humanity.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :3rd Sunday of Great Lent. Immediately after solemnly commanding us in 4 different ways to take up our cross, Jesus gave what might appear to be an out of context prophesy: "There be some of them that stand here, which shall not taste of death, till they have seen the kingdom of God come with power." This prophesy was fulfilled in part by the Transfiguration, but it is much more than that; it is the consolation that we need to take up our cross. When things are difficult, we must be viscerally aware of our the divinity and humanity of our Lord. The Gospel tells us of His divinity, and the epistle of His humanity.

item.title: 3rd Sunday of Great Lent, Sunday of the Cross. , 2014-03-22
item.description: :3rd Sunday of Great Lent. We must learn how to feel the troparion "Before Thy Cross, we bow down in worship" as we are prostrating before the cross. this is a moment in the church year that we cannot miss, when our body and soul pray as one, and the mystery of life is explained to us in words that cannot be uttered.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :3rd Sunday of Great Lent. We must learn how to feel the troparion "Before Thy Cross, we bow down in worship" as we are prostrating before the cross. this is a moment in the church year that we cannot miss, when our body and soul pray as one, and the mystery of life is explained to us in words that cannot be uttered.

item.title: 3rd Sunday of Great Lent, Sunday of the Cross, 2014-03-23
item.description: :3rd Sunday of Great Lent. We cannot understand the cross, and the way of the cross unless we understand life. What is life? Most people do not know the answer to this simple question or understand why the way of the cross is the only way to have life, or understand what is meant when we are told to "lose our life", or that everything in our life is an exchange - with life or death.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :3rd Sunday of Great Lent. We cannot understand the cross, and the way of the cross unless we understand life. What is life? Most people do not know the answer to this simple question or understand why the way of the cross is the only way to have life, or understand what is meant when we are told to "lose our life", or that everything in our life is an exchange - with life or death.

item.title: 3rd Sunday of Great Lent, 2015-03-14
item.description: :3rd Sunday of Great Lent. Explanation of why we do prostrations before the Cross, when we sing "Before Thy cross, we bow down in worship, O Master, and Thy holy resurrection, we glorify", even though prostrations on Sundays are not done on any other Sunday of the year.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :3rd Sunday of Great Lent. Explanation of why we do prostrations before the Cross, when we sing "Before Thy cross, we bow down in worship, O Master, and Thy holy resurrection, we glorify", even though prostrations on Sundays are not done on any other Sunday of the year.

item.title: 3rd Sunday of Great Lent, 2015-03-15
item.description: :3rd Sunday of Great Lent. The prostrations we do before the Cross teach us the way of life. Our basic problem is selfishness, which always leads to meaningless suffering and death, described by the Lord as "whosoever will save his life shall lose it". The way of life is submission to the will of God, described by our Lord as "taking up the cross". This way is not suffering! It is a way of joy for those who experience it in its fullness. Do the prostrations with this feeling and you will know that you can be totally free!
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :3rd Sunday of Great Lent. The prostrations we do before the Cross teach us the way of life. Our basic problem is selfishness, which always leads to meaningless suffering and death, described by the Lord as "whosoever will save his life shall lose it". The way of life is submission to the will of God, described by our Lord as "taking up the cross". This way is not suffering! It is a way of joy for those who experience it in its fullness. Do the prostrations with this feeling and you will know that you can be totally free!
